decisions.briefing: Markets sold off hard today with NASDAQ dropping 2.8% and the S&P 500 down 1.7% — a clear risk-off session. No screened candidates passed today's filters, so no new buys are warranted. Holding all six current positions; stop-losses in code will protect against any severe drawdowns automatically.

market_education.summary_en: Today's broad selloff reflects a risk-off shift where investors rotated out of growth and tech equities into safer assets like bonds and cash. A decline of this magnitude across both the NASDAQ and S&P 500 typically signals macro-level concern — often driven by factors such as hawkish Fed commentary, geopolitical escalation, or disappointing economic data. When institutional money exits risk assets simultaneously, even fundamentally strong stocks get dragged lower as funds raise cash.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日市場出現明顯的避險情緒，投資者大規模撤出科技股及成長型資產，轉而持有債券與現金等較安全的工具。納斯達克單日跌幅達2.8%、標普500指數亦下跌1.7%，此類跌幅通常反映市場對宏觀經濟前景的憂慮，常見觸發因素包括聯準會鷹派言論、地緣政治緊張局勢或經濟數據不如預期。當機構資金同步出逃時，即使基本面穩健的個股也難逃拖累，因為基金需要變現套現。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A 'risk-off' day is when investors across the market become fearful and move money out of higher-risk assets like stocks — especially growth and tech stocks — and into safer ones like government bonds, gold, or cash. It often happens suddenly in response to bad economic news or uncertainty. On days like today, even good companies fall simply because the overall mood has shifted toward caution.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「避險情緒」（Risk-Off）是指市場投資者普遍感到恐慌，將資金從股票等高風險資產撤出，轉入國債、黃金或現金等避風港的市場狀態。這種情緒轉變往往來得突然，常由負面經濟數據或不確定性引發。在這類交易日，即使是業務良好的公司股價也可能下跌，因為整體市場情緒已轉向保守謹慎。
